tests.test_body:
target datalayout = "e-p:64:64:64-p1:16:16:16-p2:32:32:32-p3:64:64:64-i1:8:8-i8:8:8-i16:16:16-i32:32:32-i64:64:64-f32:32:32-f64:64:64-v64:64:64-v128:128:128-a0:0:64-s0:64:64-f80:128:128-n8:16:32:64"

define i1 @gep_mugtiple_ugt_not_all_nuw(ptr %base, i64 %idx, i64 %idx2) {
  %gep1 = getelementptr nuw i32, ptr %base, i64 %idx
  %gep2 = getelementptr i32, ptr %gep1, i64 %idx2
  %cmp = icmp ugt ptr %gep2, %base
  ret i1 %cmp
}

target datalayout = "e-p:64:64:64-p1:16:16:16-p2:32:32:32-p3:64:64:64-i1:8:8-i8:8:8-i16:16:16-i32:32:32-i64:64:64-f32:32:32-f64:64:64-v64:64:64-v128:128:128-a0:0:64-s0:64:64-f80:128:128-n8:16:32:64"

  %gep1 = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %base, i64 %idx
  %gep2 = getelementptr nusw i32, ptr %gep1, i64 %idx2
  %cmp = icmp ugt ptr %gep2, %base
  ret i1 %cmp

issue.title: [InstCombine] Miscompilation in foldGEPICmp
issue.body:
Reproducer: https://alive2.llvm.org/ce/z/SLybDz
```
define i1 @gep_mugtiple_ugt_inbounds_nusw(ptr %base, i64 %idx, i64 %idx2) {
  %gep1 = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %base, i64 %idx
  %gep2 = getelementptr nusw i8, ptr %gep1, i64 %idx2
  %cmp = icmp ugt ptr %gep2, %base
  ret i1 %cmp
}
```
```
=>
define i1 @gep_mugtiple_ugt_inbounds_nusw(ptr %base, i64 %idx, i64 %idx2) {
#0:
  %#1 = add i64 %idx, %idx2
  %cmp = icmp sgt i64 %#1, 0
  ret i1 %cmp
}
Transformation doesn't verify!

ERROR: Value mismatch

Example:
ptr %base = pointer(non-local, block_id=1, offset=0) / Address=#x000000000000000c
i64 %idx = #x7ffffffffffffffc (9223372036854775804)
i64 %idx2 = #x0000000000000005 (5)

Source:
ptr %gep1 = pointer(non-local, block_id=1, offset=9223372036854775804) / Address=#x8000000000000008
ptr %gep2 = pointer(non-local, block_id=1, offset=-9223372036854775807) / Address=#x800000000000000d
i1 %cmp = #x1 (1)

SOURCE MEMORY STATE
===================
NON-LOCAL BLOCKS:
Block 0 >	size: 0	align: 1	alloc type: 0	alive: false	address: #x0000000000000000
Block 1 >	size: 9223372036854775804	align: 1	alloc type: 0	alive: true	address: #x000000000000000c

Target:
i64 %#1 = #x8000000000000001 (9223372036854775809, -9223372036854775807)
i1 %cmp = #x0 (0)
Source value: #x1 (1)
Target value: #x0 (0)
```
cc @nikic
issue.author: dtcxzyw

comments.author: nikic
comments.body: Probably CommonPointerBase needs to use intersectForOffsetAdd.

history.commit_subject: [InstCombine] Fold icmp of gep chain with base (#144065)
history.commit_message:
[InstCombine] Fold icmp of gep chain with base (#144065)

Fold icmp between a chain of geps and its base pointer. Previously only
a single gep was supported.
    
This will be extended to handle the case of two gep chains with a common
base in a followup.

This helps to avoid regressions after #137297.

tests.test_body:
target datalayout = "e-p:64:64-p1:16:16-p2:32:32:32-p3:64:64:64-f16:32"

%struct.x = type <{ i8 }>

@.str = external hidden constant [6 x i8]
@.str1 = external hidden constant [12 x i8]

; Function Attrs: nounwind
define i32 @test28() #0 {
entry:
  %orientations = alloca [1 x [1 x %struct.x]], align 8
  %t3 = call i32 @puts(ptr @.str) #0
  %t45 = getelementptr inbounds [1 x [1 x %struct.x]], ptr %orientations, i32 1, i32 0, i32 0
  br label %bb10

bb10:                                             ; preds = %bb10, %entry
  %indvar = phi i32 [ 0, %entry ], [ %indvar.next, %bb10 ]
  %t.0.reg2mem.0.rec = mul i32 %indvar, -1
  %t12.rec = add i32 %t.0.reg2mem.0.rec, -1
  %t12 = getelementptr inbounds %struct.x, ptr %t45, i32 %t12.rec
  %t16 = call i32 (ptr, ...) @printf(ptr nonnull dereferenceable(1) @.str1, ptr %t12) #0
  %t84 = icmp eq ptr %t12, %orientations
  %indvar.next = add i32 %indvar, 1
  br i1 %t84, label %bb17, label %bb10

bb17:                                             ; preds = %bb10
  ret i32 0
}

declare i32 @puts(ptr)

declare i32 @printf(ptr, ...)

attributes #0 = { nounwind }

target datalayout = "e-p:64:64:64-p1:16:16:16-p2:32:32:32-p3:64:64:64-i1:8:8-i8:8:8-i16:16:16-i32:32:32-i64:64:64-f32:32:32-f64:64:64-v64:64:64-v128:128:128-a0:0:64-s0:64:64-f80:128:128-n8:16:32:64"

define i1 @gep_multiple_eq(ptr %base, i64 %idx, i64 %idx2) {
  %gep1 = getelementptr i32, ptr %base, i64 %idx
  %gep2 = getelementptr i32, ptr %gep1, i64 %idx2
  %cmp = icmp eq ptr %gep2, %base
  ret i1 %cmp
}

target datalayout = "e-p:64:64:64-p1:16:16:16-p2:32:32:32-p3:64:64:64-i1:8:8-i8:8:8-i16:16:16-i32:32:32-i64:64:64-f32:32:32-f64:64:64-v64:64:64-v128:128:128-a0:0:64-s0:64:64-f80:128:128-n8:16:32:64"

define i1 @gep_mugtiple_ugt_not_all_nuw(ptr %base, i64 %idx, i64 %idx2) {
  %gep1 = getelementptr nuw i32, ptr %base, i64 %idx
  %gep2 = getelementptr i32, ptr %gep1, i64 %idx2
  %cmp = icmp ugt ptr %gep2, %base
  ret i1 %cmp
}

target datalayout = "e-p:64:64:64-p1:16:16:16-p2:32:32:32-p3:64:64:64-i1:8:8-i8:8:8-i16:16:16-i32:32:32-i64:64:64-f32:32:32-f64:64:64-v64:64:64-v128:128:128-a0:0:64-s0:64:64-f80:128:128-n8:16:32:64"

define i1 @gep_mugtiple_ugt_inbounds_nusw(ptr %base, i64 %idx, i64 %idx2) {
  %gep1 = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %base, i64 %idx
  %gep2 = getelementptr nusw i32, ptr %gep1, i64 %idx2
  %cmp = icmp ugt ptr %gep2, %base
  ret i1 %cmp
}

target datalayout = "e-p:64:64:64-p1:16:16:16-p2:32:32:32-p3:64:64:64-i1:8:8-i8:8:8-i16:16:16-i32:32:32-i64:64:64-f32:32:32-f64:64:64-v64:64:64-v128:128:128-a0:0:64-s0:64:64-f80:128:128-n8:16:32:64"

define i1 @gep_diff_base_same_indices_nuw_nusw(ptr %x, ptr %y, i64 %z) {
  %gep1 = getelementptr nuw i8, ptr %x, i64 %z
  %gep2 = getelementptr nusw i8, ptr %y, i64 %z
  %cmp = icmp ult ptr %gep1, %gep2
  ret i1 %cmp
}

target datalayout = "e-p:64:64:64-p1:16:16:16-p2:32:32:32-p3:64:64:64-i1:8:8-i8:8:8-i16:16:16-i32:32:32-i64:64:64-f32:32:32-f64:64:64-v64:64:64-v128:128:128-a0:0:64-s0:64:64-f80:128:128-n8:16:32:64"

define i1 @gep_multiple_eq_commuted(ptr %base, i64 %idx, i64 %idx2) {
  %gep1 = getelementptr i32, ptr %base, i64 %idx
  %gep2 = getelementptr i32, ptr %gep1, i64 %idx2
  %cmp = icmp eq ptr %base, %gep2
  ret i1 %cmp
}

target datalayout = "e-p:64:64:64-p1:16:16:16-p2:32:32:32-p3:64:64:64-i1:8:8-i8:8:8-i16:16:16-i32:32:32-i64:64:64-f32:32:32-f64:64:64-v64:64:64-v128:128:128-a0:0:64-s0:64:64-f80:128:128-n8:16:32:64"

define i1 @gep_mugtiple_ugt_nuw(ptr %base, i64 %idx, i64 %idx2) {
  %gep1 = getelementptr nuw i32, ptr %base, i64 %idx
  %gep2 = getelementptr nuw i32, ptr %gep1, i64 %idx2
  %cmp = icmp ugt ptr %gep2, %base
  ret i1 %cmp
}
